# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kubernetes Deployment manifests with Istio sidecar injection disabled using sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false" annotation. Skip sidecar proxies for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies. This is for services that require low latency without proxy overhead.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app # change this to your application's name if it differs from 'my_application', and vice versa, as we assume that you use lowercase for Kubernetes names. YAML cannot contain special characters such as the comma between resources or a hyphen in resource/field paths
  labels:
    app: my-app # change this to your application's label if it differs from 'my_application', and vice versa, we assume that you use lowercase for Kubernetes names. YAML cannot contain special characters such as the comma between resources or a hyphen in resource/field paths
spec: 
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }} # provide value here (default is 3), adjust if needed to suit your application's demand, e.g., number of pod instances you want for high availability and failover situations   
---  
apiVersion: v1    
kind: Service     
metadata:         
 name: my-app        # change this as per requirement i.e 'my_application', adjust if needed to suit your application's demand, e.g., service instances you want for high availability and failover situations   
  labels:          
   app: {{ .Values.AppLabel }}     # provide value here (default is same in metadata), also we assume that label values use lowercase YAML as well to adhere closely with Kubernetes naming conventions, adjust if needed according the requirement and avoid special characters such a comma or hyphen
spec:  
  type: {{ .Values.serviceType }} # provide value here (default is LoadBalancer), choose based on your application's service configuration i.e., which load balancing mode to use   
  ports:           
     - port :{{ .Values.portNumber }}, targetPort: {{ .Values.targetPort}}   #provide values as per requirement, adjust if needed e.g.: expose 'my_application' on specific Ports of Pods in service configuration and choose which type load balancing (default is LoadBalancer), based upon your application requirements i.e., whether you need it or not   
  selector:         # these should match the selectors defined under metadata   for easier selection, as per Kubernetes naming conventions according to 'my_application' and also using lowercase YAML formatting style names due to consistency across different resources/configurations. adjust if needed in accordance with your application requirements   
     app: {{ .Values.AppLabel }}   # provide value here (default is same as labels), keep it consistent according 'my_application' and avoid special characters such a comma or hyphen
